request.question: What is the middle name of the Good Morning America meteoroligist for whom Kait Parker occasionally fills in on weekends?

title: Kevin Ogle
Atlanta Braves Radio Network
Meg Oliver
Steve Grand
Tanya Rivero
Lara Spencer
Kait Parker
Rob Marciano
Chris Vlasto
Laurie Hibberd
sentences: Kevin Ogle is a news anchor for KFOR-TV (channel 4), the NBC affiliate in Oklahoma City, Oklahoma.

Kevin anchors the station's weeknight 6 and 10 p.m. newscasts with Linda Cavanaugh, does occasional reports including the "Bottom Line" financial segments seen during the station's 6:30 p.m. newscast, and occasionally fills in as anchor of the 9 p.m. newscast on Independent station KAUT-TV.
Ogle also serves as moderator of the locally-produced Sunday morning political affairs talk show "Flashpoint", alongside panelists Mike Turpen and former Oklahoma City mayor Kirk Humphreys.

The Atlanta Braves Radio Network is a 138-station network (97 A.M., 41 F.M. stations + 1 F.M. translator) heard across 10 states & one territory of the Southeastern United States that airs Major League Baseball games of the Atlanta Braves.
The flagship stations are WCNN and WYAY in Atlanta, Georgia.
The main announcers are Jim Powell and Don Sutton, who alternate between play-by-play and color commentary on each broadcast.
Ben Ingram is the pregame and postgame host and occasionally fills in on play-by-play, while Kevin McAlpin serves as a dugout reporter.
Mark Lemke provides pregame/postgame analysis and occasionally fills in for Sutton on game broadcasts.
Former known long-time announcers include Pete Van Wieren, Ernie Johnson, Sr. and Skip Caray, all deceased.
The engineer and game producer for Braves Network broadcasts is Brian "Grandma" Giffin.
Network Producers and Operators include Jonathan Chadwick, Chris Culwell, Sean Nerny, Kevin D'Amico, Brian Hoyt, Isiah Stewart and Eric Quintana.
Meg Oliver (born December 7, 1970, Birmingham, Michigan) is an award winning Network Television Correspondent and Anchor.
She returned to CBS News in 2015.

She currently reports for CBS This Morning, the CBS Evening News weekend and fills in anchoring for their 24-hour digital network by CBSN On CBSN she has covered extensive live breaking news from the San Bernardino shootings to the murders of WDBJ's Allison Parker and Adam Ward.
In March 2006, she became anchor of the overnight CBS newscast, "Up to the Minute" and remained in that position for three years.
She was also a correspondent for The Early Show, and fill in anchor.
She left CBS in 2009 and worked at ABC News as a correspondent.
She reported for Good Morning America Weekend Edition and ABC World News with David Muir.
She also filled in anchoring on World News Now.
Steve Grand (born February 28, 1990) is an American singer, songwriter and model from Lemont, Illinois, a Chicago suburb.
He became an overnight internet celebrity and the music video of his first hit "All-American Boy" went viral on YouTube in less than a week in July 2013.
This attention landed Grand on "Good Morning America", CNN and other national media.

On "Good Morning America" Steve shared how difficult it was for him to come out to his parents as a high school student—saying with obvious emotion "I felt like I was a shame to my parents and that there was no way I could ever make them proud."
Buzzfeed ranked the video on its list of the "24 Most Brilliant Music Videos from 2013", and "Out" magazine named Grand to its annual "Out100" list of the year's most compelling LGBT people.
In addition to being a musician, Grand has become an active figure in the LGBT equality movement.
He released his debut album titled "All American Boy" financed by a successful "Kickstarter" public funding campaign.
Tanya Rivero is the host of "Lunch Break" on Wall Street Journal Live since April 2014.
Until August 2013, she was anchor for ABC News Now.
Other work for ABC included hosting Good Morning America Health.
She has filed reports for Good Morning America, Nightline, World News with Diane Sawyer, Weekend World News with David Muir and anchored World News Now and America This Morning.
Before joining ABC News in October 2007, she was a reporter and fill-in anchor for WCBS-TV (CBS 2) in New York City, the flagship station of CBS Television Network.
Lara Christine Von Seelen (known professionally as Lara Spencer) (born June 19, 1969) is an American television presenter.
She is best known for being the co-anchor for ABC's "Good Morning America".
She is also a correspondent for "Nightline" and ABC News.
Previously, she was the host of the syndicated entertainment newsmagazine "The Insider" from 2004 to 2011, and was a regular contributor to CBS's "The Early Show".
Before then, she was the national correspondent for "Good Morning America" and spent several years as a lifestyle reporter for WABC-TV.
She also hosted "Antiques Roadshow" on PBS for the 2004 and 2005 seasons, and "Antiques Roadshow FYI", a spin-off of "Antiques Roadshow", during 2005.
She hosts the show "Flea Market Flip" on both HGTV and the Great American Country channel.
Kait Parker is an atmospheric scientist who currently works for weather.com and The Weather Channel app.
She can also occasionally be seen on "Good Morning America" where she fills-in for meteorologist Rob Marciano on weekends.
Prior to her role with weather.com, she was an on-camera meteorologist and host of America's Morning Headquarters Weekend Edition alongside Reynolds Wolf.
She left The Weather Channel television network in early 2016.
Robert Mark "Rob" Marciano (born June 25, 1968) is an American journalist and meteorologist who is currently employed by ABC News.

Marciano is ABC's, and currently provides forecasts for the weekend editions of "Good Morning America", a position Ginger Zee vacated when she was chosen to succeed Champion on the daily editions of "GMA".
Chris J. Vlasto (born October 27, 1966) is executive producer of Good Morning America.
Prior to that, he was a senior producer at ABC News "20/20" and senior producer of the Law and Justice unit.
Before that he was a senior Broadcast Producer of Good Morning America where he won three Emmy Awards for "Outstanding Morning Program".
He is the son of James Vlasto, former Press Secretary to the New York Governor Hugh Carey and his half brother, Josh Vlasto is currently the Deputy Communications Director to Governor Andrew Cuomo.
Hibberd began her broadcasting career as a traffic reporter, news writer, entertainment reporter and host on CKFM in Toronto from 1987 through 1992.
In the late 80s and early 90s, she also served as a host on YTV for "YTV Rocks" and "Rock'n'Talk".
She later became an entertainment reporter for WSVN-TV in Miami from 1992-1994.
She appeared on "Good Morning America" and "Good Morning America Sunday".
Before that, she co-hosted the FX cable network's morning show, "Breakfast Time", with Tom Bergeron, from 1994-1996.

candidate.summarize1.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Task: Compress the grounded first-hop evidence into a single concise, retrieval-oriented intermediate state to guide the next-hop retrieval. Do not produce the final answer or perform any new retrieval here.

Required content of `summary` (single field):
- A short header identifying the primary topic(s) found in the passages (1 line).
- A concise enumeration of the concrete facts supported verbatim by the provided passages: list entity mentions and directly supported factual spans (dates, years, titles, roles). Use very short quoted fragments (<= ~15 words) only when those words appear verbatim in the passages. Keep to facts present in the passages; do not add or infer new facts.
- Explicitly expose any ambiguity or missing information that blocks answering: if multiple candidate entities or page variants appear, enumerate those candidates and label the name as ambiguous; if a required target fact is missing or ambiguous, label it as the missing fact and identify the bridge entity that connects this hop to the next.
- Prioritized next-hop retrieval directives (1–4 items): concrete page titles or disambiguation pages to fetch, explicit article sections to inspect (e.g., lede/infobox, "Career", "Publication", "History"), or tight search keywords. Order them by priority and be specific (include possible disambiguation terms or section names).

Formatting and style constraints:
- Keep the entire `summary` concise: a short header plus 1–4 brief sentences or 2–6 short bullet lines in total.
- Preserve the habit of extracting and citing concrete facts verbatim from the passages (names, dates, passage labels).
- Do not output a final answer, comparison, or any numeric fact that purports to resolve the question here. Even if the passages fully and unambiguously contain all required facts, present that evidence and explicitly state "no additional retrieval needed," but defer producing any final answer to a downstream component.
- If a name or term has multiple distinct variants in the passages, do not pick one without disambiguating evidence; enumerate candidates instead.
- Do not invent or add facts not present in the supplied passages. Avoid long verbatim quotations; prefer short extracts (<= ~15 words) or paraphrase.

Output expectations: produce exactly one field `summary` containing the concise, retrieval-oriented summary that meets the above requirements.
candidate.create_query_hop2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

Produce exactly one compact, retrieval-focused second-hop query in the field `query`. Use only the provided `question` and `summary_1` as inputs. Do not include any explanation, answer, fallback text, lists, or additional fields — the output must be a single one-line query string.

Decision templates (choose the single template that matches the missing evidence implied by summary_1):
- Connector between two named items: emit a short interrogative naming both items and requesting the linking evidence (e.g., artist album recording) in one line. Retain the visible item tokens from the inputs.
- Metadata for a single anchor title: emit a compact keyword-style query consisting of the anchor plus brief attribute keywords (for example: artist genre).
- Temporal or aggregate fact: include the year or season token plus the entity type and a short cue such as season count.

Strict formatting rules:
- Emit exactly one line and exactly one string in `query` containing only the query text.
- Keep the query compact (aim for ≤ 10–12 words/tokens) and free of punctuation or explanatory clauses.
- Preserve visible tokens from `question` and `summary_1` (title words, years, or entity names) to anchor retrieval.
- Do not answer, conclude, enumerate candidates, assert hidden facts, or include user-facing instructions.

Produce the `query` field now.
candidate.summarize2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Instructions (must follow exactly):
- Emit a single field named `summary` containing only 1–3 concise sentences that integrate the first-hop `context` and the second-hop `passages` into a grounded post-retrieval state.
- If the `context` already contains a concise answer sentence, keep that sentence as the first sentence of `summary` unless the `passages` clearly contradict it; append supporting sentence(s) after it rather than replacing it.
- Preserve the primary answer name(s) exactly as they appear in the inputs (context or passages) unless those names clearly conflict with stronger passage content.
- When one or more `passages` supply short biographical, membership, partnership, or corroborating facts about a named entity from the `context`, append at most one brief supporting sentence per relevant named person. Each such supporting sentence should:
  - begin with the entity's canonical name as shown in the passages (if available),
  - paraphrase up to one or two key attributes present in the passages (examples: nationality, award/winner status, current affiliation/team, partner identity, founding/formation fact, birth date), and
  - avoid long verbatim copying (prefer paraphrase; use short quotes only when a brief exact phrase from a passage is necessary for clarity).
- If the question requires naming both members of a partnership or collaborator pair, name both people in the summary and, if passages provide partner-specific attributes, include one brief attribute for each as separate short clauses or sentences if needed.
- Do not invent facts not present in the provided passages. If passages conflict on a specific attribute, do not synthesize a new reconciled fact; instead prefer the passage that most directly addresses the question or omit the disputed attribute (or state necessary ambiguity only when needed to avoid a false claim).
- Keep the `summary` compact and focused: avoid extraneous biography, discography/chart data, or unrelated commentary. When `context` is absent or is not a concise answer, produce a 1–2 sentence integrated summary naming the relevant entity(ies) and including at most one brief paraphrased attribute drawn from the passages.

Output requirement: exactly one JSON field `summary` whose value is the integrated 1–3 sentence summary.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, `summary_2`, produce the fields `answer`.

Instructions:
- Use only `summary_1` and `summary_2` as the evidential basis for the final decision; do not add new facts or external information.
- First determine whether the question is a binary (yes/no) proposition and whether both `summary_1` and `summary_2` clearly and unambiguously support the same binary outcome.
- If BOTH summaries unambiguously assert the same binary conclusion, emit exactly one lowercase token as the entire content of `answer`: use "yes" for an affirmative conclusion and "no" for a negative conclusion. The `answer` field must contain that single token and nothing else (no punctuation, no extra whitespace, no explanation).
- Prefer `summary_2` as the decisive statement when it clarifies or restates `summary_1`, but still require that both summaries support the same binary judgment before emitting the single-token `answer`.
- If the summaries conflict, if either summary hedges, is ambiguous, silent about the binary relation, or otherwise fails to clearly agree, do NOT apply the single-token rule; instead produce the module's normal fallback/unresolved output for `answer`.
- Always remain truthful to the visible summaries and do not alter their factual conclusions.

response.hop1_query: What is the middle name of the Good Morning America meteoroligist for whom Kait Parker occasionally fills in on weekends?
hop1_docs: Kait Parker | Kait Parker is an atmospheric scientist who currently works for weather.com and The Weather Channel app.  She can also occasionally be seen on "Good Morning America" where she fills-in for meteorologist Rob Marciano on weekends. .  Prior to her role with weather.com, she was an on-camera meteorologist and host of America's Morning Headquarters Weekend Edition alongside Reynolds Wolf.  She left The Weather Channel television network in early 2016.

Meg Oliver | Meg Oliver (born December 7, 1970, Birmingham, Michigan) is an award winning Network Television Correspondent and Anchor.  She returned to CBS News in 2015.  She currently reports for CBS This Morning, the CBS Evening News weekend and fills in anchoring for their 24-hour digital network by CBSN On CBSN she has covered extensive live breaking news from the San Bernardino shootings to the murders of WDBJ's Allison Parker and Adam Ward.  In March 2006, she became anchor of the overnight CBS newscast, "Up to the Minute" and remained in that position for three years.  She was also a correspondent for The Early Show, and fill in anchor.  She left CBS in 2009 and worked at ABC News as a correspondent.  She reported for Good Morning America Weekend Edition and ABC World News with David Muir.  She also filled in anchoring on World News Now.

Diane Macedo | Diane Reis Macedo (born February 28, 1982) is an American news personality.  She is currently employed by ABC News where she serves as co-anchor of World News Now and America This Morning.  She also frequently fills-in as the pop news anchor on the weekend version of Good Morning America.  Before coming to ABC News, She was a weekend morning anchor for WCBS-TV in New York City.  Macedo announced her departure from WCBS on March 6, 2016.

Tanya Rivero | Tanya Rivero is the host of "Lunch Break" on Wall Street Journal Live since April 2014.  Until August 2013, she was anchor for ABC News Now.  Other work for ABC included hosting Good Morning America Health.  She has filed reports for Good Morning America, Nightline, World News with Diane Sawyer, Weekend World News with David Muir and anchored World News Now and America This Morning.  Before joining ABC News in October 2007, she was a reporter and fill-in anchor for WCBS-TV (CBS 2) in New York City, the flagship station of CBS Television Network.

Marysol Castro | Marysol Castro is an American broadcast journalist who was employed as a news anchor at WPIX in New York.  She also was a weather forecaster for "The Early Show" on CBS in 2011 and a features correspondent for the weekend edition of "Good Morning America" on ABC from 2004 to 2010.  In June 2015 Castro joined New Haven-based WTNH-TV to fill in for weekday morning traffic reports and anchor until mid-November 2015.

Rob Nelson (reporter) | Rob Nelson (born June 5, 1978) is a weekend morning anchor and weekday reporter at WABC-TV.  Nelson co-anchored the overnight news programs "World News Now" and "America This Morning" on ABC with various co-anchors, including Vinita Nair, Peggy Bunker, Tanya Rivero, Yunji De Nies, Paula Faris, Diana Perez and frequent fill-in Sunny Hostin.  Nelson left on March 15, 2013.  He originally joined ABC on July 12, 2010.  John Muller succeeded Nelson as co-anchor of "World News Now"; Nelson's enormous popularity led to him becoming a regular fill-in for Muller.  In January 2014, Nelson joined WABC-TV as co-anchor of the weekend morning newscasts, replacing Phil Lipof.

Randall Pinkston | Randall Pinkston was a correspondent/anchor for Al Jazeera America.  Previously he was with CBS News.  After a stint as a White House Correspondent in CBS's Washington Bureau, Pinkston became a general assignment reporter, contributing to CBS broadcasts, including CBS Evening News, Morning News, Weekend News, CBS News Sunday Morning and 48 Hours.  Pinkston also contributed to the CBS Reports documentary, Legacy of Shame with Correspondent Dan Rather.  Pinkston has filled in as anchor on the CBS Evening News-Weekend Edition, Up to the Minute and CBS Morning News.
response.summary_1:
Primary topic: Rob Marciano (Good Morning America meteorologist)

- From passages: Kait Parker "fills-in for meteorologist Rob Marciano on weekends." (passage [1])
- From passages: Kait Parker is "an atmospheric scientist" and "occasionally be seen on 'Good Morning America'." (passage [1])
- Missing fact: Rob Marciano's middle name is not present in the supplied passages — bridge entity: Rob Marciano.
- Prioritized next-hop retrievals:
  1. Fetch the "Rob Marciano" Wikipedia page — inspect lede/infobox and "Early life"/"Personal life" sections for full name/middle name.
  2. If no page, search for "Rob Marciano full name" or "Rob Marciano middle name" (news bios, ABC/Good Morning America staff bios).
  3. Also try variants: "Robert Marciano" or "Roberto Marciano" (include "(meteorologist)" in query) to locate formal birth name.
hop2_docs: Rob Marciano | Robert Mark "Rob" Marciano (born June 25, 1968) is an American journalist and meteorologist who is currently employed by ABC News.  Marciano is ABC's, and currently provides forecasts for the weekend editions of "Good Morning America", a position Ginger Zee vacated when she was chosen to succeed Champion on the daily editions of "GMA".

Marciano Art Foundation | The Marciano Art Foundation (formally named the Maurice and Paul Marciano Art Foundation) is a non-profit arts foundation located on Wilshire Boulevard in the Mid-Wilshire neighborhood of Los Angeles, California.  It was established by the co-founders of Guess, Maurice Marciano and Paul Marciano.  The Marcianos plan to open it on May 25, 2017 as an exhibition space to display their 1,500-piece collection of contemporary art.

Marciano Stores | Marciano Stores is an American upscale fashion brand, which is owned and operated by Guess Inc named after Guess's CEO Paul Marciano.  It is the first extension line by Guess and includes clothing such as tops, skirts and dresses, jeans and accessories such as necklaces, earrings and bracelets sold exclusively in Marciano and selected Guess stores.
Roc Marciano | Rakeem Calief Myer (born February 11, 1978), better known by his stage name Roc Marciano, is an American rapper, songwriter, and producer.
Marciano | Marciano is both a surname and a given name.  It originates from Latin "Marcianus" or "Marcian" (Saint Marcians) or "Martians" or "Martianus".  Also from the cult of Roman god Mars.
Léo Marciano | Léo Marciano Paris is a French luxury fashion house founded in 1970 by the eponymous designer Léo Marciano.
Marciano Bruma | Marciano Bruma (born as Marciano van Homoet on 7 March 1984) is a Dutch footballer who currently plays for XerxesDZB in the Dutch Hoofdklasse.
response.summary_2: Kait Parker occasionally fills in on weekends for Good Morning America meteorologist Rob Marciano. Robert Mark "Rob" Marciano's middle name is Mark.
